pr.title: Match the first functions of lbfile and lbarchive
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the first functions in the lbfile and lbarchive modules by moving small assembly regions into new C source/header files, wiring those source objects into the build, and renaming callsites from anonymous func_/lbl_ symbols to lbFile_/lbArchive_ names. lbArchive_InitializeDAT was fully expressed in C and replaces func_80016A54 across lbarchive, lbdvd, efasync, and text_2 callsites. lbFile_8001615C was expressed in C, while lbFile_800161A0 retained an inline assembly fallback under the matching path with a NON_MATCHING C version documented. The lbfile sbss symbol lbl_804D63C8 was renamed to cancel.
what_changed: Added src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c implementing lbArchive_InitializeDAT(HSD_Archive* archive, u8* data, u32 length).
Added src/melee/lb/lbarchive.h with the lbArchive_InitializeDAT prototype and archive/type includes.
Removed the assembly body for func_80016A54 from asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.s and replaced all shown callsites with lbArchive_InitializeDAT.

lbArchive_InitializeDAT calls HSD_ArchiveParse, reports and asserts on parse failure, then iterates extern symbols with HSD_ArchiveGetExtern and relocates them with HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern until NULL.
Added src/melee/lb/lbfile.c implementing lbFile_8001615C in C and providing lbFile_800161A0 as inline assembly for matching, with a NON_MATCHING C equivalent.
Added src/melee/lb/lbfile.h declaring lbFile_8001615C and lbFile_800161A0.
Removed the original assembly bodies for lbl_8001615C and func_800161A0 from asm/melee/lb/lbfile.s.
Renamed lbfile callback references from lbl_8001615C to lbFile_8001615C and polling calls from func_800161A0 to lbFile_800161A0.
Renamed the sbss variable lbl_804D63C8 to cancel in assembly and include/variables.h.
Updated include/functions.h comments to reflect lbFile_800161A0 and lbArchive_InitializeDAT names.
Added src/melee/lb/lbfile.o and src/melee/lb/lbarchive.o to obj_files.mk before their corresponding asm object files.
Added lbfile.o and lbarchive.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
smart_moves: Introduced C source objects before the remaining asm objects in obj_files.mk, allowing only the matched leading functions to move out of assembly while preserving the rest of the module in asm.
Renamed every visible branch target/callsite to the new semantic symbol names, improving cross-module readability without needing to decompile all consumers.
Kept lbFile_800161A0 matching via inline assembly while recording the intended simple C behavior under NON_MATCHING, a useful compromise when the obvious C form does not yet match.
Used exact existing string/data labels for OSReport and __assert inputs rather than inventing new string definitions, minimizing data-layout risk.
Created module headers immediately with prototypes, making future decompilation units able to include lbfile/lbarchive APIs instead of relying on global function stubs.
decomp_lessons: For partially decompiled modules, split only the matched leading functions into src/ and leave the remaining assembly in asm/; object ordering can preserve addresses while incrementally shrinking assembly.
When a decompiled function is widely called from assembly, update asm call comments/labels to the semantic name so future reviewers can identify call graph meaning.
Small callback/polling functions may require inline asm even when their C logic is trivial; preserving the NON_MATCHING C version documents intent without sacrificing matching.
Archive-loader functions can often be understood from library calls: parse archive, assert on failure, iterate externs by index, locate each extern, terminate on NULL.
Renaming a global sbss symbol should be propagated consistently through assembly, C extern declarations, and include/variables.h to avoid mixed anonymous and semantic names.
naming_conventions: Semantic library/module prefixes were introduced: lbArchive_ for archive routines and lbFile_ for file routines.
The known archive initializer was named lbArchive_InitializeDAT instead of leaving the address-style func_80016A54.
Unidentified-but-module-scoped functions retained the address suffix convention, such as lbFile_8001615C and lbFile_800161A0.
The anonymous sbss label lbl_804D63C8 was renamed to the descriptive lowercase global cancel.
Header guards used lowercase file-style names: _lbarchive_h_ and _lbfile_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted matched assembly bodies for func_80016A54 and lbl_8001615C after adding equivalent C implementations.
Retained lbFile_800161A0 as an inline asm function in the matching build while placing the natural C implementation behind #ifdef NON_MATCHING.
Used nofralloc in the inline asm body for lbFile_800161A0 to preserve the exact hand-written prologue/epilogue sequence.
Preserved branch instruction encodings in asm files while changing symbolic call labels to lbArchive_InitializeDAT and lbFile_800161A0.
Kept existing rodata/sdata labels as extern char arrays for assert/report strings instead of moving or redefining data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile lbFile_800161A0 without inline asm.
Search for subsequent renames of lbFile_8001615C to a semantic callback name.
Look for later PRs that continue decompiling asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.s after lbArchive_InitializeDAT.
Check whether cancel remains a global BOOL or is later folded into a struct/state object.
Compare later lbarchive headers for more complete HSD_Archive API typing.
